title: Rubus gyamdaensis
intro: Rubus gyamdaensis, also known as Gyamda raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rubus gyamdaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus gyamdaensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to relieve symptoms of colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Rubus gyamdaensis is a species of raspberry in the family Rosaceae. It is a type of bramble native to the Himalayas. The exact classification of Rubus gyamdaensis can be further broken down into its genus and species, with the genus being Rubus and the species being gyamdaensis. The genus Rubus is a large and diverse group of plants that includes various species of raspberries, blackberries, and dewberries. Further classification may also include its subgenus or section within the genus.
